STM32H7 (Cortex-M7 @ 480MHz).notes: High-performance MCU. The M7 core used with Genx320 at IISc. Best STM32 for ML.

STM32F7 (Cortex-M7 @ 216MHz).notes: Mid-range Cortex-M7. Good for audio and vision ML tasks.

STM32F4 (Cortex-M4 @ 168MHz).notes: Workhorse of embedded ML. Has FPU. Widely supported by X-CUBE-AI and TFLite Micro.

STM32L4 (Cortex-M4 @ 80MHz).notes: Ultra-low power M4. Good for battery-powered ML inference.

STM32G4 (Cortex-M4 @ 170MHz).notes: Newer M4 variant with DSP extensions. Good for signal processing + ML.

STM32F1 (Cortex-M3 @ 72MHz).notes: Very constrained. No FPU. Avoid neural networks unless extremely small.

ESP32-S3 (Xtensa LX7 @ 240MHz).notes: Has vector extensions for ML. The RISC-V adjacent chip used at IISc. Good IoT ML chip.

ESP32 (Xtensa LX6 @ 240MHz).notes: No FPU. Float ops are software-emulated and slow. INT8 strongly recommended.

ESP32-C3 (RISC-V @ 160MHz).notes: RISC-V core. Lower power than ESP32. Limited ML capability without FPU.

Raspberry Pi Pico (Cortex-M0+ @ 133MHz).notes: Popular and cheap. No FPU on M0+. Good for simple TFLite Micro models.

Nordic nRF52840 (Cortex-M4 @ 64MHz).notes: BLE-focused MCU with M4F. Used in wearable ML applications.

Nordic nRF9160 (Cortex-M33 @ 64MHz).notes: LTE-M/NB-IoT modem + M33 core. Good for cellular IoT with on-device inference.

Silicon Labs EFM32GG (Cortex-M4 @ 48MHz).notes: Ultra-low energy MCU. Simplicity Studio target. Good for energy-harvesting ML nodes.

Arduino Nano 33 BLE (Cortex-M4 @ 64MHz).notes: Popular TinyML platform. Runs TFLite Micro. Good entry-level embedded ML board.

Arduino Uno (ATmega328P @ 16MHz).notes: Almost no ML capability. Listed for reference only — avoid neural networks here.
